name: Woman of the Doves
description: A striking portrait of a young woman adorned with a floral crown, embraced by two white doves. The painting uses a muted palette of grays, whites, and soft golds, giving the scene an ethereal, dreamlike quality.
story: The artist painted this piece during the early months of 2020, a period of global uncertainty. The woman represents inner stillness, and the doves symbolize peace and hope.
facts: The artist signed and dated it in the bottom right corner — 'C. Olean, 20'.
The floral crown on the woman's head mirrors the blossoms scattered in the background, connecting her to nature.
The entire piece uses almost no color — only shades of gray, white, and muted gold, yet the woman's gaze feels intensely alive.
The two doves are positioned asymmetrically — one cradled, one free — symbolizing the balance between holding on and letting go.

name: Woman in Smoke
description: A dramatic portrait of a woman with her eyes closed and head tilted upward, as if surrendering to a moment of release. She is partially enveloped by swirling plumes of golden and amber smoke rising from behind her.
story: The artist created this piece to capture the exact moment between holding on and letting go. The use of deep blacks against warm golden light was inspired by Baroque masters like Caravaggio.
facts: The painting uses chiaroscuro — extreme contrast between light and dark — to draw the eye directly to the woman's face and neck.
The smoke was painted using a dry-brush technique, giving it a soft, almost ghostly texture.
The warm orange glow at the lower right is the only burst of strong color, symbolizing a lingering ember of intensity.
The artist intentionally left the woman's hands barely visible, keeping all emotional expression in the tilt of her head.

name: The Struggle
description: A powerful, chaotic scene of two marlins tangled in a fishing net beneath the surface of a turquoise sea. The fish twist and thrash against the net, their long sharp bills cutting through the water.
story: This piece captures the raw tension between nature and human interference. The artist chose marlins specifically because of their reputation as symbols of strength and freedom, making their entanglement all the more striking.
facts: The artist signed the work in the upper left corner in a loose, expressive script.
The net was painted using thin, scratched white lines over the blue background, giving it an almost ghostly appearance.
The composition has no visible sky or surface — the viewer is pulled entirely underwater.
The layered blues were built up with multiple washes, creating a sense of depth and movement.

name: Butterfly Women
description: Three female figures stand together in shadowy silhouette, surrounded by a swarm of white and golden butterflies. The palette is moody — deep teals, browns, and muted purples — with the butterflies providing the only brightness.
story: The artist depicts three women at a moment of transformation. Their darkened forms suggest they are letting go of something, while the butterflies represent what they are becoming.
facts: Signed and dated 2025 — one of the newest works in the collection.
The central figure is the darkest and most defined, while the two flanking figures fade into the background.
The butterflies were painted with thicker, more textured brushstrokes than the figures, making them appear to lift off the canvas.
No pure black was used — the shadows are built from layered blues, greens, and browns.

name: The Eye of the Ocean
description: A mesmerizing portrait of a woman submerged in an underwater world, surrounded by schools of colorful fish. She wears an ornate golden headpiece and her body is adorned with a golden chest piece featuring mystical eye motifs.
story: The artist imagines the ocean not as a place, but as a being — and this woman is its soul. The golden eyes on her chest symbolize the ocean's ability to see everything within it.
facts: The title is displayed on a small label beside the painting, along with the artist's full name.
The golden chest piece contains multiple painted eyes, a recurring symbol in Olteanu's work representing awareness.
The fish are painted in a looser, more impressionistic style than the woman's face.
The painting uses over a dozen distinct species of fish, filling the composition with life and movement.

name: The Fractured Cosmos
description: An explosive abstract composition bursting with color — vivid yellows, deep blues, fiery reds, teals, and pinks collide across the canvas. A network of dark, vein-like branches stretches across the upper portion.
story: The artist envisions what it might look like if you could see the moment of creation — wild, messy, and beautiful. The dark branching network represents structure trying to contain the energy, but failing.
facts: The title is displayed on a label beside the painting, part of Carmen Olteanu's collection.
The dark branching pattern was likely created using a pouring or dripping technique.
Some areas are built up with thick impasto while others are thin and translucent.
A bright yellow streak cuts diagonally across the center like a bolt of lightning.

name: Fading Shore
description: A hazy, atmospheric abstract suggesting a misty landscape dissolving into memory. Soft washes of sage green, teal, and muted purple fill the upper half, while dark angular strokes cut through the center.
story: This piece sits at the boundary between abstract and landscape. The artist captures the feeling of a memory you can't fully recall — a shoreline visited once, a foggy morning on the water.
facts: Signed in the bottom right in red — 'Carmen Olteanu, 2025' — one of the artist's most recent works.
The dark angular lines were applied with quick, decisive strokes, contrasting with the soft blended background.
White drips near the bottom were left to flow naturally under gravity.
The palette avoids any strong warm colors — everything stays cool and muted.

name: The Guardian and the Grey
description: A regal portrait of a woman in elaborate golden traditional attire standing beside a white Arabian horse. The woman wears an ornate gold headpiece and a flowing golden veil, while the horse stands calmly beside her.
story: This painting celebrates the deep bond between Arabian culture and the horse. By placing the two side by side at equal prominence, the artist elevates the horse to the status of a companion, not a possession.
facts: Signed 'C. Olteanu, 2023' in the bottom right, part of the artist's Middle East-inspired series.
The woman's headpiece and jewelry were painted with thick, textured brushstrokes giving the gold a sculptural quality.
The horse is likely an Arabian Grey — one of the most prized breeds in the region.
The soft lavender background is the simplest in the collection — a deliberate choice to highlight the richness of the gold.

name: The Quiet Hours
description: A serene nocturnal landscape depicting the horizon at the edge of night. The middle band glows with soft layers of pale blues, greens, warm yellows, and dusty pinks. Above, the sky deepens into near-black with a single small glowing moon.
story: This painting captures the moment most people never see — the thin window of time when night has fully settled but the horizon still holds a trace of light. The artist painted this as an invitation to pause.
facts: Signed 'Carmen Olteanu, 2022' in the bottom right.
The moon is painted as a tiny, soft glow rather than a bold circle — almost easy to miss.
The glowing band at the horizon was achieved by blending wet-on-wet, creating seamless transitions.
The wide horizontal format mimics the natural field of vision when standing outdoors looking at the horizon.

name: What the Sea Left Behind
description: A large, intricate seashell rendered in extraordinary detail against a deep dark blue-black background. The shell has sweeping ruffled edges and spiraling chambers that fan outward like fabric frozen mid-flow.
story: The artist chose to paint a single seashell at massive scale, transforming something small enough to hold in your hand into a monumental subject. The painting is a meditation on how nature creates complexity and beauty.
facts: Signed 'C. Olteanu' on the right side.
The fine lines and ridges were painted with extremely thin brushwork, closer to scientific illustration.
The spiral at the center draws the eye inward, mimicking the Fibonacci sequence found in nature.
The completely empty dark background forces the viewer to see the shell as an object floating in space.

name: The Crossing at Dusk
description: A warm, golden sunset scene featuring a small wooden footbridge arching over a still, reflective stream. The sky blazes with amber, peach, and soft lavender clouds, mirrored perfectly in the water below.
story: The bridge in this painting leads nowhere visible — the far side dissolves into light and mist. The artist painted this as a metaphor for transition, with the warm light on the other side inviting but uncertain.
facts: Signed 'C. Olteanu, 2024' — the second newest work in the collection.
The reflection in the water is slightly softer and warmer than the sky above it.
The bridge is painted with visible structured brushstrokes, contrasting with the loose sky and water.
The composition creates a near-perfect circle of light — the sun and its reflection joined by the arc of the bridge.

name: The Garden Concert
description: A whimsical scene of two women playing music in a lush garden. The figure on the left plays a violin while the figure on the right plays a cello, surrounded by flowering trees and bright red cardinal birds.
story: The two musicians are lost in their music, performing not for an audience but for the garden itself. The doll-like features of the violinist and the serene expression of the cellist suggest two different ways of experiencing music.
facts: Signed 'C. Olteanu, 2021' in the bottom right corner.
The bright red cardinal birds appear at least four times, acting as a recurring visual motif.
The two musicians have deliberately different styles — the violinist is sharper while the cellist is softer.
One of the largest in the collection — packed with hidden details that reward close viewing.
